name: Mixed Figma comments — tests classification + priority

comments.author: @sarah
comments.text: The spacing between cards feels too tight

comments.author: @mike
comments.text: Can we try a purple color scheme instead of blue?

comments.author: @sarah
comments.text: Users in testing couldn't find the save button — 3/5 failed the task

comments.author: @alex
comments.text: Can we add a bulk export feature?

comments.author: @mike
comments.text: The loading spinner overlaps the header on slow connections

expectedBehaviors: Asks user to choose feedback source (A/B/C/D) — user picks B (Figma comments)
Pulls comments and presents them in a table with author, text, resolved status
Filters to unresolved comments only (5 of 6)
Classifies each correctly: spacing=principle, purple=preference, save button=usability, export=scope, overlap=bug
Prioritizes: save button (usability) > spacing (principle) > overlap (bug) > purple (preference, needs decision) > export (deferred as scope change)
Does NOT apply the purple color change without asking — it's preference-based
Flags the export feature as a scope change for /plan-design, not a revision
Applies the save button fix using Fitts's Law (larger, more prominent)
antiPatterns: Applies all feedback without classifying first
Changes the color to purple without asking (preference = ask first)
Tries to implement the bulk export (scope change = defer)
Treats all feedback as equal priority
Doesn't present the classification table before acting

evaluationCriteria.classificationAccuracy: Are all 5 comments classified into the correct feedback type?
evaluationCriteria.priorityOrder: Does it apply usability > principle > bug > preference > scope?
evaluationCriteria.preferenceHandling: Does it ASK about the purple change instead of just doing it?
evaluationCriteria.scopeRecognition: Does it flag export as a scope change and defer it?
